script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'serac-plan-consistency-audit',
  description: 'Audit plan + mockup + backlog for internal contradictions and drift against the final agreed Serac workflow-viewer design',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Audit', detail: 'parallel: plan contradictions, mockup validity+fidelity, backlog+cross-artifact consistency' },
    { title: 'Synthesise', detail: 'dedupe + prioritise a single fix list' },
  ],
}

const SPEC = `FINAL AGREED DESIGN (ground truth — flag anything in the artifacts that contradicts THIS):
1. Workflows and Agent Teams are NOT separate sidebar sections. Each is an ordinary SESSION CARD in the one normal card list (a workflow/team IS its parent session).
2. The WF/team tag sits on the card's SECOND metadata line (id · model · TAG), NOT on the title. It appears only when the session has agents beneath it. Reuse the .worktree-count-chip quiet outlined look.
3. The roll-up (progress bar + phase/member counts + token/tool metrics) and a NEUTRAL "View workflow"/"View agent team"/"View subagents" pill appear only when the session has agents. The bar is the primary "has agents" signal.
4. NEUTRAL styling for the View pills and the detail-panel header chips — NO per-type colour (no teal-for-workflow / blue-for-team). Text carries the distinction. Only functional status colours (running/done dot+bar) remain.
5. Card body click -> open the parent/invoking conversation via existing claude-vscode.editor.open session-open (team: orchestrator leadSessionId; workflow: parent session owning workflows/wf_<runId>.json).
6. The View pill -> a DETAIL NAVIGATION PANEL: two-pane (left = phase/roster/flat navigator depending on source; right = the selected agent's transcript rendered INLINE and scrollable). ONE component keyed by source: 'workflow' | 'team' | 'subagents'. Reuses transcriptRenderer parsing fed into the webview (NOT writing a .md and opening an editor tab for the drill-in).
7. The panel opens as an editor tab via ViewColumn.Beside, REUSING the existing right-hand editor group; it does NOT spawn a new pane, and the parent conversation is the user's already-open left editor.
8. Agent list (nav) scrolls independently. OPEN/DEFERRED question: reader horizontal space (~320px side column wraps long responses) — fallback is a full-width viewer in the bottom panel; not decided.
9. Archive uses the NORMAL session archive path: archived workflow/team show as today's COMPACT archive rows with only the WF/team tag trailing the label. No "archived" chip, no special section, no full dimmed-card treatment.
10. serac.workflowInlineThreshold and the inline-vs-top-level-section split are DROPPED.
11. Detail data: completed workflows from the sidecar workflows/wf_<runId>.json (Tier 1); running workflows reconstructed (Tier 2, flat fallback); teams from config.json + transcripts; subagents from <session>/subagents/agent-<id>.jsonl.
12. Backlog items: Cornice deprecation (firm), loops, schedules (spike), process-liveness, team workspace-scoping, unified click-through, View-subagents generalisation.

FILES:
- Plan: /Users/murraystubbs/.claude/plans/radiant-enchanting-clover.md
- Mockup: /Users/murraystubbs/repos/snowmeltio/serac/mockups/workflow-team-views.html
- Backlog: /Users/murraystubbs/repos/snowmeltio/serac/BACKLOG.md
- Serac real CSS (for fidelity): /Users/murraystubbs/repos/snowmeltio/serac/media/panel.css`

const FINDINGS_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
  properties: {
    findings: { type: 'array', items: {
      type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
      properties: {
        where: { type: 'string', description: 'file + section/line/quote' },
        problem: { type: 'string', description: 'the contradiction, stale decision, or error' },
        fix: { type: 'string', description: 'concrete correction' },
        severity: { type: 'string', enum: ['high', 'medium', 'low'] },
      }, required: ['where', 'problem', 'fix', 'severity'],
    }},
    summary: { type: 'string' },
  }, required: ['findings', 'summary'],
}

phase('Audit')
const [plan, mockup, backlog] = await parallel([
  () => agent(`Audit the PLAN file ONLY for internal contradictions and decisions that are now STALE vs the final agreed design. Read it fully and check every section against the spec. Pay special attention to leftover language from superseded decisions (e.g. "top-level section", "inline threshold", per-type pill colours, "open a .md transcript", "new pane", title-tag). \n\n${SPEC}`,
    { phase: 'Audit', label: 'audit:plan', agentType: 'Explore', schema: FINDINGS_SCHEMA }),
  () => agent(`Audit the MOCKUP HTML for (a) HTML/CSS validity (unclosed tags, broken rules), (b) fidelity to Serac's REAL palette/classes in media/panel.css, and (c) whether it correctly reflects EVERY point of the final agreed design (tag on meta line not title; neutral pills; archive as compact rows with trailing tag; two-pane navigation panel; ViewColumn.Beside framing). Read both the mockup and media/panel.css.\n\n${SPEC}`,
    { phase: 'Audit', label: 'audit:mockup', agentType: 'Explore', schema: FINDINGS_SCHEMA }),
  () => agent(`Audit the BACKLOG file for correctness, AND check CROSS-ARTIFACT consistency: do the plan, mockup, and backlog agree on the final card model, archive treatment, pill neutrality, panel behaviour, and the dropped threshold? Read all three files. Flag any place where two artifacts disagree.\n\n${SPEC}`,
    { phase: 'Audit', label: 'audit:backlog+xref', agentType: 'Explore', schema: FINDINGS_SCHEMA }),
])

phase('Synthesise')
const synthesis = await agent(`Consolidate these three audit reports into ONE deduped, severity-ordered fix list for the maintainer. Drop duplicates, merge overlapping items, and separate "must fix (contradicts the agreed design)" from "nice-to-have". Be concrete about file + location + the exact edit.\n\n[PLAN AUDIT]\n${JSON.stringify(plan, null, 2)}\n\n[MOCKUP AUDIT]\n${JSON.stringify(mockup, null, 2)}\n\n[BACKLOG+XREF AUDIT]\n${JSON.stringify(backlog, null, 2)}`,
  { phase: 'Synthesise', label: 'synthesis', schema: {
    type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
    properties: {
      mustFix: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { artifact: {type:'string'}, where: {type:'string'}, problem: {type:'string'}, fix: {type:'string'} }, required: ['artifact','where','problem','fix'] } },
      niceToHave: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { artifact: {type:'string'}, where: {type:'string'}, fix: {type:'string'} }, required: ['artifact','where','fix'] } },
      overallConsistent: { type: 'boolean' },
      verdict: { type: 'string' },
    }, required: ['mustFix', 'niceToHave', 'overallConsistent', 'verdict'],
  }})

return { plan, mockup, backlog, synthesis }

findings.problem: Describes the original superseded design: 'small workflows treated like subagents (inline, click-through to each agent's view), large ones drillable from a dedicated top-level section.' This contradicts the final agreed design where ALL workflows/teams are uniform cards in the same list with no inline-vs-section split.
findings.fix: Delete line 5 and replace with: 'Murray wants to monitor these runs in the Serac sidebar as ordinary session cards that surface agent progress and click-through to per-agent transcripts via a unified detail panel.'

findings.problem: States 'Threshold (inline vs drilldown) keys off the cheap `agentCount` field.' This references the now-dropped inline-vs-threshold split (serac.workflowInlineThreshold). The final agreed design has NO threshold; all cards are uniform, and the View pill always appears when agents exist.
findings.fix: Delete 'Threshold (inline vs drilldown) keys off the cheap `agentCount` field.' The agentCount field is still read for card UI logic (determining whether to show the roll-up bar and View pill), but there is no inline/drilldown branching based on it.

findings.problem: Says the detail view right-pane renders transcript 'inline and scrollable.' While contextually this means 'within the webview,' the wording is confusing given the dropped inline-vs-section split. More critically, this section mischaracterizes the old approach: 'The drill-in is NOT "render a `.md` and open it in an editor tab" (the original §6 approach).' Section 6 IS the final agreed design; this phrasing suggests section 6 was a superseded option.
findings.fix: Clarify: 'right = the selected agent's transcript rendered within the webview reader pane (prompt/assistant/tool/result turns, scrollable). The panel opens via ViewColumn.Beside (editor tab beside the conversation), not as a separate .md file.'

findings.problem: Test instruction: 'panel.integration.test.ts for inline-vs-drilldown at the `agentCount` threshold.' This test expects branching logic where agentCount determines inline vs drilldown rendering. The final agreed design has no such threshold—all cards are uniform.
findings.fix: Replace 'inline-vs-drilldown at the `agentCount` threshold' with 'roll-up bar and View pill visibility when agentCount > 0, and detail panel opening on pill click.' agentCount still drives UI visibility logic but not rendering path branching.

findings.problem: Manual test instruction: 'large run shows summary row + top-level section, small run inlines.' This expects different rendering for large vs small runs, contradicting the uniform card design where all cards render identically with roll-up bar and View pill when they have agents.
findings.fix: Replace with: 'both large and small runs show the same card + roll-up bar + View pill layout; click the pill to open the detail panel.' Remove the 'large shows top-level section' / 'small inlines' distinction.

findings.problem: Test instruction: 'A session with no workflows is visually unchanged (section + inline group absent).' This phrasing implies Workflows and Subagents were separate UI sections/groups. The final agreed design has no separate section—workflows are just ordinary cards in the single card list.
findings.fix: Replace with: 'A session with no workflows shows only normal cards in the card list; plain session cards without agents do not show the roll-up bar or View pill.'

plan.summary: The PLAN contains 5 significant contradictions against the final agreed design. Three are HIGH severity: (1) the Context section still describes the old superseded design with inline-vs-top-level split; (2) Tier 1 description still references the dropped threshold logic; (3) test instructions still expect inline-vs-drilldown branching. Two are MEDIUM severity: clarifying the detail-panel description which conflates 'inline within webview' with the dropped inline-rendering strategy, and fixing the test instruction that implies separate Workflows/Subagents sections existed. All are stale language from the original proposal that survived the 2026-06-03 design decision. The core design (cards, discovery, panel component) correctly implements the final agreed design; only these documentation passages contradict it.

mockup.summary: Comprehensive audit of workflow-team-views.html mockup against final agreed design and real Serac CSS. The mockup is structurally sound and correctly implements the two-pane detail panel, archive layout, and tag placement on the metadata line. However, there are critical class name mismatches: the mockup's `.tag` and `.chip` classes are custom-defined instead of reusing `.worktree-count-chip` from panel.css (per explicit design requirement). Additionally, several CSS properties use hardcoded values instead of Serac's vscode-aware variables (palette tokens, backgrounds, transitions, borders). Tag text should be 'WF' (uppercase) not 'wf'. Archive class names are abbreviated (.arow, .aage) instead of matching real panel.css (.compact-row, .compact-age). Most fixes are straightforward CSS class reuse and variable substitution; all 10 design points are correctly reflected in HTML structure.

findings.problem: Mockup defines custom `.tag` and `.chip` classes instead of reusing `.worktree-count-chip` from panel.css (lines 406-421). The agreed design explicitly says 'reuse the .worktree-count-chip quiet outlined look' for both the meta-line WF/team tag AND the detail-panel header chips. The mockup's custom styles diverge from the real palette.
findings.fix: Delete the custom `.tag` class definition (lines 30-35). Delete the custom `.chip` class definition (line 59). Replace all instances of class="tag" with class="worktree-count-chip" and all class="chip" with class="worktree-count-chip". This ensures both tags and header chips inherit the real outlined-quiet appearance from panel.css:406-421.

findings.where: mockup line 126: tag text 'wf' should be 'WF'
findings.problem: The mockup shows lowercase `wf` but the agreed design refers to 'WF' (uppercase abbreviation). The team tag on line 116 correctly shows 'team' in lowercase, but WF should be uppercase per the design specification.
findings.fix: Change `wf` to `WF` on line 126.

findings.where: mockup lines 48-53: Archive section class names
findings.problem: Archive class names are abbreviated (.arch-h, .arow, .al, .aage) and don't match real panel.css naming conventions. Real CSS uses .archive-header (line 486), .compact-row (line 652), .compact-name, .compact-age. This inconsistency makes it harder to trace implementation to the real CSS.
findings.fix: Rename: .arch-h → .archive-header, .arow → .compact-row, .al → .compact-name, .aage → .compact-age. Update HTML to use the longer, real class names. Verify archived workflow/team rows use the same compact styling as defined in panel.css lines 486-493 and 652-657.

findings.problem: The mockup imports only a subset of Snowmelt palette tokens (--sm-blizzard-blue, --sm-snow-blue, --sm-teal-bright, --sm-blizzard-blue-rgb). Real panel.css (lines 2-17) defines the full palette. Partial import creates inconsistency and makes future palette updates risky.
findings.fix: Import all palette variables from panel.css lines 2-17. This ensures full fidelity and propagates any real palette changes to the mockup.

findings.where: mockup line 23: .card background color is hardcoded
findings.problem: Uses `background:#232323` instead of `background: var(--vscode-editor-background, #2d2d2d)` as in real panel.css:265. Hardcoded value doesn't respect VS Code light/dark theming or user customization.
findings.fix: Change `background:#232323` to `background: var(--vscode-editor-background, #2d2d2d)`. For transitions, use `transition: background 0.1s` to match real panel patterns (line 271).

findings.where: mockup line 45: .typepill border color is hardcoded
findings.problem: Uses `border:1px solid #3a3a3a` with hardcoded dark color that won't adapt to light theme. Neutral pill styling should be theme-aware per Serac conventions.
findings.fix: Change to `border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.15)` for dark theme. Add light-theme override: `body.vscode-light .typepill { border-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.15); }`. This matches Serac's pattern for neutral interactive elements (similar to `.ws-main-chip` line 130 in real CSS).

findings.problem: Uses `background:#2a2a2a` instead of VS Code's hover state variable. Should use `var(--vscode-list-hoverBackground)` like real panel.css:530 for theme consistency.
findings.fix: Change `.typepill:hover { background: var(--vscode-list-hoverBackground, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); }` to inherit Serac's hover pattern.

findings.where: mockup line 21: .sc.done color uses hardcoded hex instead of variable
findings.problem: Uses `color:#7acdd0` (teal-bright) directly. Though this matches --sm-teal-bright (panel.css:10), hardcoding the hex value reduces maintainability and clarity.
findings.fix: Change `color:#7acdd0` to `color:var(--sm-teal-bright)` for consistency with other color references in the mockup. This makes the intended palette choice explicit.

findings.where: mockup line 29: .status class naming and padding divergence
findings.problem: Mockup uses `.status { padding:1px 6px; border-radius:8px; }` but real panel.css:349 defines `.status-pill { padding:2px 6px; border-radius:10px; }`. Class names and proportions differ.
findings.fix: Rename `.status` to `.status-pill` and update padding to `2px 6px` and border-radius to `10px` for fidelity to real CSS.

findings.where: mockup line 120 and 130: View pills use custom glyphs (⧉, ▦)
findings.problem: Mockup shows `⧉ View agent team` and `▦ View workflow` with custom Unicode glyphs. Agreed design emphasizes that text carries distinction (neutral pills). Glyphs may not render consistently across systems.
findings.fix: Simplify to plain text: 'View agent team' and 'View workflow' without glyphs. The text itself provides semantic distinction. Keep the arrow (→) as an affordance for clickability.

findings.where: mockup lines 114, 124: HTML title attributes document behavior
findings.problem: Uses `title=` attributes as mockup annotations ('click card → talk to the orchestrator'). These are preview documentation, not part of the real implementation, and clutter the HTML.
findings.fix: Remove `title=` attributes from the HTML. The behavior is wired in TypeScript, not HTML attributes. Documentation can live in code comments or design docs.

findings.problem: Archive treatment contradicts ground truth. Backlog describes archived teams/workflows as 'stay in place, dimmed, with a quiet trailing chip' + reopen affordance; ground truth (plan section 9, mockup line 97) specifies archived items move to the normal archive section as compact rows, identical to today, with no in-place dimming or separate section.
findings.fix: Revise line 20: remove 'stay in place, dimmed' language. Replace with: 'Archived workflow/team items appear in the normal archive section as compact rows with only the WF/team tag trailing the label, identical to archived sessions. No separate section, no in-place dimming. Archived teams keep config.json on disk; TeamDelete stays a separate, deliberate action (never wired to X).'

findings.problem: Backlog lists Cornice deprecation as firm, but the plan does not propose Cornice deprecation in any section—only workflows + native Agent Teams are in scope. This is scope addition without plan consensus.
findings.fix: Remove Cornice deprecation from backlog, OR get explicit plan sign-off that Cornice should be deprecated as part of this work. Current state: undecided and not reflected in the plan.

findings.problem: Missing explicit closure: the setting `serac.workflowInlineThreshold` has been dropped per plan section 9 ('No new setting required. serac.workflowInlineThreshold is dropped'). The backlog references the dropped split as context but does not list the setting deprecation as a closure/decision item.
findings.fix: Add to Deprecations section: 'Drop serac.workflowInlineThreshold setting. No inline-vs-section split; workflow/team cards render uniformly in the normal card list. Remove from package.json, src/settings.ts, and CLAUDE.md.'

findings.where: BACKLOG.md, line 14 (Subagent view > View subagents pill)
findings.problem: Backlog lists View subagents as a separate post-workflow item ('Lands after the workflow viewer establishes'). Plan section 8 treats it as v1: 'the panel is one component keyed by source: workflow | team | subagents'. Both arrive together since they share the same panel implementation. This is a scope/sequencing mismatch.
findings.fix: Either move View subagents into the v1 plan (it ships with the panel, not after), OR get explicit plan sign-off to defer. Current backlog wording implies deferral, but plan expects v1 delivery as one unified navigation panel.

backlog.summary: Four cross-artifact inconsistencies found. High severity: BACKLOG line 20 contradicts ground truth on archive treatment (dimmed in-place vs moved to archive section). Three medium-severity scope/sequencing mismatches: Cornice deprecation added to backlog without plan mention; workflowInlineThreshold setting deprecation missing from backlog closure items; View subagents sequenced as fast-follow in backlog but expected in v1 per plan. Mockup and CSS are consistent with plan; plan and mockup agree on all card anatomy, tag placement, neutral chip styling, and archive layout.

mustFix.where: Context section, line 5 (and the dependent stale phrasings at lines 35, 174, 176, 177 that all flow from the same superseded inline-vs-section design)
mustFix.problem: The plan still describes the original superseded design — 'small workflows treated like subagents (inline, click-through to each agent's view), large ones drillable from a dedicated top-level section.' The final agreed design (plan's own sections 8 and 10, lines 102-110) is that ALL workflows/teams render as uniform ordinary session cards in the single card list, with NO inline-vs-section split and NO threshold. Line 5 is the root contradiction; it is internally inconsistent with lines 102-110 of the same document.
mustFix.fix: Replace line 5's final sentence ('Murray wants to monitor these runs ... per-agent transcripts.') with: 'Murray wants to monitor these runs in the Serac sidebar as ordinary session cards that surface agent progress and click-through to per-agent transcripts via a unified detail panel.' Remove the 'small ... inline / large ... top-level section' clause entirely.

mustFix.where: Tier 1 description, line 35: 'Threshold (inline vs drilldown) keys off the cheap `agentCount` field.'
mustFix.problem: References the dropped inline-vs-threshold split. The plan itself (lines 107, 110) states the split and serac.workflowInlineThreshold are dropped and cards are uniform. agentCount is still read, but only for card UI visibility (roll-up bar + View pill), not for a rendering-path branch.
mustFix.fix: Delete the sentence 'Threshold (inline vs drilldown) keys off the cheap `agentCount` field.' Optionally replace with: 'agentCount is read for card UI logic (whether to show the roll-up bar and View pill), with no inline/drilldown branching.'

mustFix.where: Verification, line 174: 'panel.integration.test.ts for inline-vs-drilldown at the `agentCount` threshold and `viewWorkflowAgent` emission.'
mustFix.problem: The prescribed test asserts inline-vs-drilldown branching keyed on an agentCount threshold — logic the final design removed. Tests written to this instruction would encode the dropped behaviour.
mustFix.fix: Replace 'inline-vs-drilldown at the `agentCount` threshold' with 'roll-up bar and View pill visibility when agentCount > 0, and detail panel opening on pill click'. Keep the '`viewWorkflowAgent` emission' clause.

mustFix.where: Verification, manual step, line 176: 'large run shows summary row + top-level section, small run inlines.'
mustFix.problem: Manual test expects different rendering for large vs small runs, contradicting the uniform-card design (lines 102-107).
mustFix.fix: Replace with: 'both large and small runs show the same card + roll-up bar + View pill layout; click the pill to open the detail panel.' Remove the 'large shows top-level section / small inlines' distinction.

mustFix.where: Custom .tag class (line 35) and custom .chip class (line 59); usages at lines 116, 126, 153-154 (.tag) and 172, 192 (.chip)
mustFix.problem: The mockup defines bespoke .tag and .chip styles instead of reusing .worktree-count-chip from media/panel.css:406-421. The agreed design (plan lines 103, 155) explicitly requires reusing the .worktree-count-chip quiet-outlined look for BOTH the meta-line WF/team tag AND the detail-panel header chips. Verified: panel.css:406-421 defines worktree-count-chip and panel.css:422-424 has the vscode-light override — the custom classes bypass both, so they will not match real theming.
mustFix.fix: Delete the .tag rule (line 35) and the .chip rule (line 59). Replace every class="tag" with class="worktree-count-chip" and every class="chip" with class="worktree-count-chip".

mustFix.problem: WF abbreviation is rendered lowercase; the agreed design and the mockup's own card-anatomy legend (lines 97, 161) refer to it as uppercase 'WF'. ('team' staying lowercase at line 116 is intentional and correct.)
mustFix.fix: Change the text 'wf' to 'WF' on line 126. Also update the archived row at line 153 (wf → 'WF') for consistency.

mustFix.where: Archive class names at lines 49-53 (.arch-h, .arow, .al, .aage) and their HTML usages at lines 152-155
mustFix.problem: Abbreviated class names diverge from real panel.css naming, obscuring the implementation trace. Verified real names: .archive-header (panel.css:486), .compact-row (652), .compact-name (656), .compact-age (657).
mustFix.fix: Rename .arch-h → .archive-header, .arow → .compact-row, .al → .compact-name, .aage → .compact-age in both the <style> block (lines 49-53) and the HTML (lines 152-155), so archived workflow/team rows reuse the real compact styling.

mustFix.problem: Contradicts ground truth on archive treatment. Backlog says archived teams/workflows 'stay in place, dimmed, with a quiet trailing chip after the name'. The plan (line 107) and mockup (lines 151-155) specify archived items move into the NORMAL archive section as compact rows, identical to today, with the WF/team tag trailing the label — no in-place dimming, no separate section.
mustFix.fix: Rewrite line 20: remove 'stay in place, dimmed' and the 'rather than a separate Archived section' framing. Replace with: 'Archived workflow/team items appear in the normal archive section as compact rows with only the WF/team tag trailing the label, identical to archived sessions — no in-place dimming, no separate section. A dismissed team keeps its config.json + task list on disk; offer reopen that resumes the orchestrator session. TeamDelete stays a deliberate, separate action, never wired to the X (which archives).'

niceToHave.where: Detail-panel section, line 141: 'right = the selected agent's transcript rendered inline and scrollable' and 'NOT "render a `.md` ..." (the original §6 approach)'
niceToHave.fix: Clarify the wording so 'inline' is not confused with the dropped inline-vs-section split, and fix the misleading '§6' reference (section 6 is the final design, not a superseded option — the markdown/editor-tab path is the FALLBACK, per line 143). Suggested: 'right = the selected agent's transcript rendered within the webview reader pane (prompt/assistant/tool/result turns, scrollable). The panel opens via ViewColumn.Beside, not as a separate .md file (the .md/editor-tab path remains only as the whole-session fallback).'

niceToHave.where: Verification, line 177: 'A session with no workflows is visually unchanged (section + inline group absent).'
niceToHave.fix: The '(section + inline group absent)' parenthetical implies separate Workflows/Subagents sections existed, contradicting the single-card-list design. Replace with: 'A session with no workflows shows only normal cards; plain session cards without agents show no roll-up bar and no View pill.'

niceToHave.where: Line 14, 'Subagent view > View subagents pill' — 'Lands after the workflow viewer establishes the shared panel.'
niceToHave.fix: Resolve the sequencing mismatch with the plan. Plan lines 145 and 102-105 treat View subagents as v1 (the panel is one component keyed by source: workflow|team|subagents, 'build once, reuse three ways'). Either move View subagents into v1 in the backlog to match the plan, or get explicit sign-off to defer it and add a one-line note to the plan recording the deferral. Pick one and make both docs agree.

The plan does not propose Cornice deprecation — plan line 113 places the new Workflows doc subsection 'next to Cornice', implying Cornice stays. The backlog entry is marked 'Confirmed 2026-06-03' but is absent from the plan's scope. Either add a Cornice-deprecation line to the plan (Changes section) to legitimise it, or annotate the backlog entry as out-of-plan-scope / separate work so the two artifacts stop disagreeing.

Add an explicit closure item for the dropped setting: 'serac.workflowInlineThreshold is dropped — cards render uniformly, no inline-vs-section split (per plan sections 8-9).' NOTE: I verified the setting does NOT exist in package.json or src/settings.ts, so this is a documentation/decision-record closure only — do NOT instruct removal from code. The only code/doc touchpoint is ensuring CLAUDE.md never reintroduces it.

niceToHave.where: Hardcoded theme values: .card background #232323 (line 23); .typepill border #3a3a3a (line 45) and :hover background #2a2a2a (line 46); .sc.done color #7acdd0 (line 21); .status padding/radius (line 29)
niceToHave.fix: Substitute vscode-aware variables to match real panel.css: .card background → var(--vscode-editor-background, #2d2d2d) (panel.css:265); .typepill border → rgba(255,255,255,0.15) with a body.vscode-light override; .typepill:hover background → var(--vscode-list-hoverBackground, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)) (cf. panel.css:530); .sc.done color → var(--sm-teal-bright) (panel.css:10); rename .status → .status-pill with padding 2px 6px and border-radius 10px (panel.css:344-350). Also import the full Snowmelt palette (panel.css:2-17) rather than the partial subset at mockup lines 8-9.

niceToHave.where: View pill glyphs at lines 120 ('⬡ View agent team') and 130 ('▦ View workflow'); title= annotation attributes at lines 114, 124
niceToHave.fix: Drop the custom Unicode glyphs (note: the team glyph is actually ⬡, not ⧉ as one source report stated) — the agreed design says neutral pills where text carries the distinction; keep the trailing → affordance. Remove the title= mockup-annotation attributes (behaviour is wired in TypeScript, not HTML).

synthesis.verdict: Three artifacts (PLAN at /Users/murraystubbs/.claude/plans/radiant-enchanting-clover.md, MOCKUP at mockups/workflow-team-views.html, BACKLOG at BACKLOG.md) carry residual contradictions against the final 2026-06-03 agreed design. Consolidated to 8 must-fix and 7 nice-to-have items after deduping and merging overlaps. All file paths and cited line numbers were verified against the actual files. MERGES/DEDUPES: (1) The PLAN's five stale-language findings (lines 5, 35, 174, 176, 177) all stem from one dropped concept (inline-vs-section split / agentCount threshold); kept as four must-fix because each is a distinct concrete edit, with line 5 flagged as the root cause and line 177 demoted to nice-to-have since it is wording-only. (2) The 'archive treatment' finding appears in both the PLAN-adjacent review and the BACKLOG xref — they agree, so merged into one BACKLOG line-20 must-fix (the PLAN line 107 is already correct). CORRECTIONS to the source reports: (a) workflowInlineThreshold does NOT exist in package.json or src/settings.ts — verified — so the backlog closure is documentation-only; do not instruct code removal. (b) The mockup's team View-pill glyph is ⬡, not ⧉ as one report quoted. (c) Mockup CSS line-number references to panel.css all check out (worktree-count-chip 406-421, status-pill 344-350, archive-header 486, compact-row/name/age 652-657). SEVERITY SPLIT: must-fix = contradicts the agreed design or will mislead implementation/tests (uniform-card design violations, wrong class reuse, wrong tag case, archive-treatment contradiction). nice-to-have = clarity, theming fidelity, cosmetic glyphs, and scope/sequencing decisions (Cornice deprecation and View-subagents timing) that need a sign-off call rather than a mechanical edit.
